column3: Info/Notes

(2004 - 2012)
This is an umbrella era for any song that isn’t on any album, while Ace was either still uploading music to his MySpace, Hypebeast & imeem accounts, or just whenever Ace was last used.

(2006 - 2007)
At Your Own Risk is another one of Tyler's earliest projects and was set to release on MySpace in 2008. This project contains a lot of reworked beats from Dreams In 3-D such as I Dream, Air Balloon, & Lounges X Blox. This was also mainly an instrumental album, with the only songs actually having vocals being Get Fresh with Burtono & 12:01 with MP. Burtono has or had this entire album on a CD.  This is one of three of Tyler's earliest projects that we have in full.
Instrumental to "12:01," used at the beginning of the "Bored Freestyle / Odd Toddlers Freestyle".
Instrumental to "Get Fresh" that's speculated to be the non-remix version Tyler mentioned in the cover (shoutout abe & Skoolie300).
Instrumental used in the "Prototype Freestyle", also called "Sorry...".
Instrumental to "Speakerboxx," released sometime in 2006 or 2007, as indicated by a poll on MySpace asking people to choose their favorite songs.

(2006 - 2008)
Stereo-Type is a mixtape that was set to release in 2008. Supposedly this entire project had vocals, but all that has leaked from this project are instrumentals (apart from I Dream & Speakerboxx, these are from that copy). This vocal copy of the project is currently lost. Made alongside Radio Arcade.
Early version of the "7-Up" instrumental, differences are unknown.
Instrumental to "7-Up".

(2007 - 2011)
Poly/Mono is another one of Tyler's aliases, described as being "Ghetto-Tech" with "808s And Shit." (This was the description of one of the Poly/Mono albums). Has multiple songs with Matt Martians. Multiple projects would be made under this name, all of them being somewhat lost now. Also most of these songs were going to be used for Adventure Time due to an interview Tyler did. This is an umbrella era for any Poly/Mono song that isn't on any album.
Instrumental to "Spunk" that Tyler made while he was in school.

Ace project that was teased alongside Stereo-Type, thought to be a Poly/Mono project but isn't one. Shares similarities with Stereo-Types artwork.
First version of the "Mushrooms" instrumental, differences unknown.
Second version of the "Mushrooms" instrumental.

(2007 - 2010)
Personal Stash was a playlist Tyler made that contained some of his favorite beats from around 2007 - 2010. It's unlikely that anything else tagged with this will surface again, as these are files taken directly from Tyler's personal emails.

Instrumental to "Fresher Than This" by Skoolie300. Skoolie confirmed that Tyler made this beat for him on the spot and then recorded it same day.

(2007 - 2008)
Relatively obscure project that was really only shared around with friends, quite a bit of this project was made at school. The songs on this project were tagged with many different artists, those being: Tyler, The Creator, Ace, Casey Veggies (CaseyV.), Skoolie300 (Skool Boy), brandUn DeShay, T Moody, & K Mak. This project's last modified date is May 13th, 2008, but some songs were made later than that. This is one of three of Tyler's earliest projects that we have in full.

This is likely to be an early version of The Odd Future Tape Vol. 1 or a solo version of said project. Teased for release in early 2008 by Tyler on his blogspot.

(2008 - 2009)
The Senioritis Tape is a collaboration tape with Tyler & Odd Future that was planned to release when they graduated high school and was most likely scrapped soon after it was announced. Barely any information known about it other than it holding some OF Tape Vol. 1 instrumentals. It was annonced on the Odd Future Blogspot on June 6th, 2009 and was supposed to be released on June 18th, 2009.
Instrumental to "Fucking Lame" off of The Odd Future Tape, Vol.1.

(2008 - 2009)
III TV Cab Thief is the semi-predecessor to Dinosaur, mostly comprised of instrumentals with the exception of "Fuck Santa." Mentioned on April 8th, 2008. Anything made in after April in 2008 and before February of 2009 that isn't tagged with any other album goes in this era.

Instrumental to "Nose Bleed" that was played on Loiter Squad.

(2008 - 2009)
Dinosaur is a Tyler project that was set to release in March 2009 before being delayed, then scrapped in fall of the same year for Bastard. Tyler has stated on Twitter in 2010 that this project later became Bastard & that he still had a bunch of rough drafts from "what it would have been."
Instrumental to "Asthma", recorded on by Tyler and Earl. Made in 2009.

Zombie Circus is a Tyler project that was started in late 2008, and worked on until mid 2009 where it later became WOLF [V1]. This album has been mentioned on a few of Tyler's songs. Tyler stated in an interview in 2013 that Zombie Circus would be releasing soon, but this interview has now been deleted. The base image for this era's album cover was later used on Bastard as an alternate cover.

Instrumental to "Rotten Sarah," made in 2009. The comment for this song states "Gonna Write Some Dark Shit To This... Sarah... Forever..."
Instrumental to an alternate version of "Wolf," has some unheard parts.

(2009)
Bastard is Tyler's first mixtape and his debut studio album, released on December 25, 2009. An updated version of this album was released on the same day in 2010. Likely started in September of 2009.

Radical is the second mixtape by Odd Future, released on May 7, 2010. This whole project consists of Odd Future members rapping over other people's instrumentals. Also notable for being the first time the EarlWolf name would be used.
Instrumental to "Orange Juice" by EarlWolf, leaked alongside other Earl material. Also the instrumental to "Lemonade" by Gucci Mane.

(2009 - 2010)
Rolling Papers is Domo Genesis's debut mixtape, released on August 30th, 2010. This era mainly contains a bunch of beats used on the project.

(2008 - 2011)
Original version of Wolf, dubbed "WOLF 2010," that was worked on during Bastard and was meant to release alongside Goblin as a mixtape with both full songs and instrumentals. Eventually, the album was delayed for Tyler to focus on Goblin, but this was still being worked on. None of the songs from this iteration of the album are known to have made it onto the final version of Wolf. While yes, the era does span from 2008 - 2011, Supersonic [V2] is the ONLY "WOLF [V1]" tagged song that's 2008. This project started around January - February 2009 & was supposedly finished by November 11th, 2010.
Session file for Half/Half, what's linked is a snippet of the session being played & a screenshot of the Logic Pro app with the song so you could easily remake this if you had everything.

WOLF [V1] track, planned to be the intro to the project at some point during its development but was scrapped sometime in late 2009. Mentioned by Tyler but all direct mentions of this are currently lost, as indicated by someone asking about the song and if it was still the intro.
Reworked version of the instrumental to "Bitches Brewin'". Meant for the rerecorded version of the song.

Instrumental to "Chewbacca", made sometime in 2009.

Finished version of the "Fish" instrumental. Thought to have leaked over 10 years ago, but it turns out it was a fake made from the open parts of Fish [V2].

Final version of "Haack", recorded on for "In The Sun". This version features some bridges, additional production and is longer by a few seconds. It's named after electronic musician Bruce Haack.

Rough draft of the Tron Cat Instrumental, made in 2010.
Final version of the instrumental to Tron Cat, made in 2010. Possibly unmixed and doesn't have the outro like in other versions.
Instrumental to "Unorthodox," made on May 1st, 2009. As of right now, this is all we have of the lost track. Leaked on March 8th, 2025 after a $330 groupbuy.
Instrumental for the slowed down version of "Wolf" that Tyler recorded on.
Acapella to "Good Morning" that was being sold with the instrumental.

(2009 - 2011)
Goblin is Tyler's second studio album, released on May 10, 2011. This album is an attempt to expand upon the style that Bastard had, but falls short. Even Tyler himself says this is his worst album.
GarageBand project file for "Analog" with a Frank Ocean feature.
Version of the "Analog" instrumental, only described as "The Other Version Of Analog Instrumental". Differences unknown.
Instrumental to "Analog" that was made during the Bastard 2 period, played during Nuts + Bolts. This version of the instrumental is unpitched, and is missing some sound effects.
Instrumental to "Boppin' Bitch," made during the Bastard 2 period.
Earlier version of the instrumental to Her off of Goblin, doesn't contain the intro.  Made in 2009-2010
Instrumental to "Hey You" that is slightly different from the one used in the song, different sound effects can be heard in the snippets.
Instrumental to "Radicals" that was made in 2009.
Instrumental to "Sandwitches." Made in 2010.
Instrumental to "She" that Tyler mentioned in a tweet, said to have been with Snoop Dogg in mind but he was unable to send the beat over.

According to the Ye tracker, Tyler sent the beat for "SMUCKERS" to Kanye West and Jay-Z in 2011 for Watch The Throne, they were actually going to use it before being taken back and later using it for Cherry Bomb. Likely never recorded on, but atleast Kanye recorded on the final version.
Instrumental to "Toilet Milk" that is shorter and higher pitched than the one in the final song, reused for The OF Tape Vol. 2 & Wolf [V2].
Instrumental to "WSUP" that's unpitched and longer.

Instrumental to "Wheels 2", snippet played during a VC.

(2011 - 2012)
Nilbog is the name of a Tyler project that he did while he was healing from a foot injury he got at the House of Blues on July 16th, 2011, but also a bit before the release of Goblin. This album was not posted publically but screenshots were posted and immediately deleted, showing to have 37 tracks. First tracklist of Nilbog is length determined. Second tracklist is yet to be found.
Instrumental to "Analog 2" that was confirmed to be on Nilbog, doesn't contain the 4-count intro, nor the vox. Made before July as Tyler recorded Analog 2 [V1] on this.
Instrumental to "Baby (Tyler, The Creator Remix)" that has a higher tempo and without an outro.
Original instrumental to "Samuel's Song," was going to be reused on WOLF [V2].

(2011 - 2013)
The Dead Sams is a Tyler project that was first mentioned on Wolf and scrapped in late 2013, the concept for this project was conceived (or at least mentioned) in the Freeperm archive on June 7th, 2012. This project is said to sound like "early Toro stuff and shitty N.E.R.D.," as well as "all jazz chords but like garage rock." Also to be noted that The Dead Sams is the name of Tyler's "dream band," in which they would do covers of songs.
Original version of Awkward made for The Dead Sams, made sometime in January 2012.

OG Filename: Lost Weekend INSTRUMENTAL
Instrumental to the original version of "Colossus."
Original version of Cowboy made for The Dead Sams, possibly made sometime in 2011.
Confirmed to be from The Dead Sams, wildly different from the rough draft & release version but has resemblence to the final.

Confirmed to be from The Dead Sams, mentioned on the Freeperm archive alongside lyrics to the song in August 2012. Said to be about Salem leaving "Camp Nilbog", and Tyler and Slater being sad. Track 8 on the project. Tyler is heard adlibbing "Salem". Was recorded over.
Original version of 48's instrumental with an alternate intro, played in the Wolf Documentary. Interupted by Domo23 snippet, OG file hasn't leaked yet.
Original version of "See You Again," made in 2013 & posted to Vine by Hodgy (with a video of a dinosaur humping a car).
The Instrumental to the title track of Wolf [V3], originally from The Dead Sams. Differences are unknown.

(Late 2011 - Mid 2012) (as Tyler, The Creator)
After the release of Goblin, Tyler would continue his work on WOLF, reusing some material from the original while making new pieces of work.
Original version of the "Awkward" instrumental with no switch up around the third verse, unpitched. Was used as the instrumental to the imfamous "Sprung" fake.

(2012 - 2013)
Wolf is Tyler's third studio album, released on April 1, 2013. This album is a big departure from his earlier work because he didn't want to be talking about the same stuff he used to, "Talking about rape and cutting bodies up, it just doesn't interest me anymore... what interests me is making weird hippie music for people to get high to."

column2:
WANG$AP
(Collaboration with A$AP Rocky)
(DON'T TAP THE GLASS)
column3:
(2017 - 2019)
WANG$AP is the planned collaboration album between Tyler & A$AP Rocky. While working on “Flower Boy," Tyler and A$AP Rocky had the idea to create a collaboration album where they would bring their talents together. In late 2018, the two released a single called ‘Potato Salad’. Although this is the only official release we ever got from this project, there were more songs made. Tyler stated in early 2019 on Twitter “i wish rocky would shut the fuck up and stop always saying what he's working on 40 years before its even a fleshed out idea.” and “that shit dont exist yall =)” when asked about WANG$AP. Tyler later revealed in 2025 that him and Rocky as a duo were originally meant to be called "DON'T TAP THE GLASS", a phrase which he would eventually reuse as the name of his ninth studio album.

column2: CALL ME IF YOU GET LOST [V1]
column3:
(2019 - 2020)
Originally, CALL ME IF YOU GET LOST would've been a mixtape where he would rap over other people's beats, but he would later scrap this idea. This idea can be seen mostly with the throwaways in this era, but also in some songs on the album such as LUMBERJACK & SIR BAUDELAIRE. Tyler mentions in an interview that he initially thought the album was done in November 2020, but he would later rework the project. Was initially thought to be called HOUSE ON FIRE, but this was later disproven.

(2020 - 2024) (2025)
CHROMAKOPIA is Tyler's eighth studio album, released on October 28th, 2024. This album is the first album by Tyler, The Creator to break many of his "traditions" when it comes to releasing albums: he broke his 2 year rule (a rule where he only drops an album every two years, The Estate Sale dropped just last year) & his 10th track rule (when there are two or three tracks in one track, specifically on the 10th track of the album). This album was worked on as far back as 2020 alongside CALL ME IF YOU GET LOST, which means Tyler spent extra time with this one.

DROP THE INSTRUMENTAL ALBUM TY

(2024 - 2025)
DON'T TAP THE GLASS is Tyler's ninth studio album, released on July 21st, 2025. This project started on December 31st, 2024 and all of the verses were recorded in May 2025 during the CHROMAKOPIA tour. It was first teased on July 16th with a photo of him with a trumpet in a marching band outfit, this is where fans got the idea that this was either for the Sticky music video that was teased months prior or a CHROMAKOPIA deluxe. On July 18th, the album's physicals were released on the Golf Wang store, which is how the fans got their first taste of the album.
